message:
test(fuzz): add ratelimit_headers fuzz target for HTTP rate-limit header parser

Adds a libfuzzer harness for fcp-ratelimit::RateLimitHeaders::parse and the
per-provider variants. Tracks bead flywheel_connectors-8714g.

Three pieces, wired together:

1. fuzz/Cargo.toml
   Adds fcp-ratelimit as a path dep and registers the fuzz_ratelimit_headers
   bin (test=false, doc=false, bench=false matching every other entry).

2. fuzz/fuzz_targets/ratelimit_headers.rs (new)
   Drives RateLimitHeaders::parse plus the per-provider variants (GitHub,
   Twitter, Stripe, OpenAI, Anthropic) and Provider::parse_headers on
   adversarial HashMap<String, String> header maps that exercise:
   - oversized integer values for X-RateLimit-{Limit,Remaining,Reset},
     including u32::MAX boundaries and negative-looking inputs;
   - Retry-After variants — purely numeric, RFC-2822 HTTP-date, garbage,
     negative, post-9999 dates;
   - duplicate header keys at different ASCII cases (the lookup is
     case-insensitive — fuzzer must not surface a panic on the iter scan);
   - whitespace, embedded NUL, very long values, non-UTF-8 already
     filtered by the String shape but extreme byte sequences remain;
   - random Provider selection so cross-shape interactions are exercised.

   Invariants asserted across every input:
     1. Parser never panics.
     2. is_limited() is always callable on the result without panic.
     3. The parsed struct is Clone/Debug-safe (touch both).

3. fuzz/Cargo.lock
   Materializes fcp-ratelimit (and a transitive edge through one of its
   helper crates) into the fuzz workspace lockfile so reproducer corpora
   stay pinned to the exact resolved graph.

No production source paths in fcp-ratelimit were touched — this is purely
a coverage-guided harness over the existing public API.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
